behavioral_patterns.value:
Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Obtain exposed email records from data breach
  • Step 2: Combine email addresses with passwords from previous breach databases
  • Step 3: Test credential pairs against enterprise portals, VPNs, and identity providers at scale using automation

Detection: Monitor for multiple failed login attempts from same source IP against different accounts, or successful logins with credentials from known breach databases
behavioral_patterns.context: Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain

behavioral_patterns.value:
AI-assisted targeted phishing campaign

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Process exposed email list through AI tooling
  • Step 2: Generate personalized phishing messages referencing the target organization
  • Step 3: Tailor lures using job title, department, or LinkedIn data
  • Step 4: Send visually indistinguishable fake correspondence

Detection: Detect email campaigns with organizational references sent shortly after breach announcements, or emails with suspicious sender reputation but high visual similarity to legitimate templates

Help desk social engineering attack

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Gather valid email address and basic OSINT on target
  • Step 2: Call IT support impersonating the employee
  • Step 3: Request password reset, MFA device reset, or account unlock
  • Step 4: Bypass technical authentication through human process

Detection: Monitor help desk requests for authentication resets, especially when caller cannot provide standard verification information or requests are made outside normal business hours

Real-time phishing relay (AiTM) attack

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Set up reverse proxy between victim and legitimate service
  • Step 2: Forward victim credentials to real site in real time
  • Step 3: Relay MFA challenge from real site to victim
  • Step 4: Forward victim's MFA response through proxy
  • Step 5: Capture authenticated session token

Detection: Detect authentication sessions with unusual network paths, timing anomalies between credential submission and MFA response, or sessions originating from proxy infrastructure

MFA fatigue attack

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Obtain valid user credentials
  • Step 2: Trigger repeated push notifications to user's device
  • Step 3: Continue bombardment until user approves out of frustration
  • Step 4: Gain authenticated access through approved session

Detection: Monitor for multiple consecutive MFA push notifications to same user, especially outside normal work hours or when user hasn't initiated login attempts

Cloud-synced passkey compromise chain

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Target user's cloud account recovery mechanisms
  • Step 2: Execute SIM swap attack against recovery phone number
  • Step 3: Use compromised phone to reset cloud account
  • Step 4: Access synced passkeys through compromised cloud account

Detection: Monitor for SIM swap indicators followed by cloud account recovery attempts, or passkey usage from new devices after account recovery events

Device theft to authentication bypass

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Steal or gain physical access to user's device with stored passkeys
  • Step 2: Attempt to use device-bound passkeys
  • Step 3: Bypass biometric verification through device compromise or stored templates
  • Step 4: Authenticate using stolen device credentials

Detection: Detect authentication attempts from devices in unusual locations, or biometric authentication patterns that differ from established user behavior

snowflake_sql:
-- KTLYST Detection Rules
-- Generated from 6 rules
-- ============================================

-- Rule 1: Detect Behavior: Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain


-- ID: IOC-BEHAVIOR-cb7b8dc0
-- Generation: template (success)
-- Description: Detects behavior indicator 'Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Obtain exposed email record' extracted from threat intelligence.
-- ------------------------------------------------------------
-- Behavioral Pattern Detection: Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Obtain exposed email record
-- Source: behavioral_0
-- TTP: T1078
-- Kill Chain: defense-evasion
-- Type: unknown

SELECT
    case_id,
    user_id,
    case_notes,
    case_description,
    created_at,
    'Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Obtain exposed email record' AS matched_pattern,
    'T1078' AS ttp_code,
    'behavioral_0' AS source_span
FROM case_notes
WHERE (
    REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_notes), LOWER('\\bEmail\ breach\ to\ credential\ stuffing\ attack\ chain\
\
Behaviors:\
\ \ •\ Step\ 1:\ Obtain\ exposed\ email\ record\\b'))
    OR REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_description), LOWER('\\bEmail\ breach\ to\ credential\ stuffing\ attack\ chain\
\
Behaviors:\
\ \ •\ Step\ 1:\ Obtain\ exposed\ email\ record\\b'))
)
AND created_at >= DATEADD(day, -90, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP())
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

-- Rule 2: Detect Behavior: department
-- ID: IOC-BEHAVIOR-2ad29f65
-- Generation: template (success)
-- Description: Detects behavior indicator 'department' extracted from threat intelligence.
-- ------------------------------------------------------------
-- Behavioral Pattern Detection: department
-- Source: behavioral_1
-- TTP: T1566
-- Kill Chain: 
-- Type: unknown

SELECT
    case_id,
    user_id,
    case_notes,
    case_description,
    created_at,
    'department' AS matched_pattern,
    'T1566' AS ttp_code,
    'behavioral_1' AS source_span
FROM case_notes
WHERE (
    REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_notes), LOWER('\\bdepartment\\b'))
    OR REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_description), LOWER('\\bdepartment\\b'))
)
AND created_at >= DATEADD(day, -90, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP())
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

-- Rule 3: Detect Behavior: MFA device reset
-- ID: IOC-BEHAVIOR-73c4d250
-- Generation: template (success)
-- Description: Detects behavior indicator 'MFA device reset' extracted from threat intelligence.
-- ------------------------------------------------------------
-- Behavioral Pattern Detection: MFA device reset
-- Source: behavioral_2

    case_id,
    user_id,
    case_notes,
    case_description,
    created_at,
    'MFA device reset' AS matched_pattern,
    'T1621' AS ttp_code,
    'behavioral_2' AS source_span
FROM case_notes
WHERE (
    REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_notes), LOWER('\\bMFA\ device\ reset\\b'))
    OR REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_description), LOWER('\\bMFA\ device\ reset\\b'))
)
AND created_at >= DATEADD(day, -90, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP())
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

-- Rule 4: Detect Behavior: or sessions originating from proxy infrastructure
-- ID: IOC-BEHAVIOR-0a0d0266
-- Generation: template (success)
-- Description: Detects behavior indicator 'or sessions originating from proxy infrastructure' extracted from threat intelligence.
-- ------------------------------------------------------------

-- TTP: T1566

    case_id,
    user_id,
    case_notes,
    case_description,

AND created_at >= DATEADD(day, -90, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP())

compiled_outputs.sentinel:
// KTLYST Detection Rules (KQL for Microsoft Sentinel)
// Generated: 7 rules from 7 indicators
// ============================================================

// Rule 1: Behavioral Detection - Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1078
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 2: Behavioral Detection - AI-assisted targeted phishing campaign
// Category: initial_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1566
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
| where AttachmentCount > 0 or UrlCount > 0
| summarize EmailCount = count(),
    UniqueRecipients = dcount(RecipientEmailAddress),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by SenderFromAddress, Subject
| where EmailCount >= 3
| sort by EmailCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 3: Behavioral Detection - Help desk social engineering attack
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1621
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 4: Behavioral Detection - Real-time phishing relay (AiTM) attack
// Category: initial_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1566
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
| where AttachmentCount > 0 or UrlCount > 0
| summarize EmailCount = count(),
    UniqueRecipients = dcount(RecipientEmailAddress),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by SenderFromAddress, Subject
| where EmailCount >= 3
| sort by EmailCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 5: Behavioral Detection - MFA fatigue attack
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1078
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 6: Behavioral Detection - Cloud-synced passkey compromise chain
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 7: Behavioral Detection - Device theft to authentication bypass
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1078
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100
compiled_outputs.splunk:
``` KTLYST Detection Rules (SPL for Splunk)
``` Generated: 7 rules from 7 indicators

``` Rule 1: Behavioral Detection - Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1078
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 2: Behavioral Detection - AI-assisted targeted phishing campaign
``` Category: initial_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1566
index=main sourcetype=mail action=delivered (attachment_count>0 OR url_count>0)
| stats count as email_count, dc(recipient) as unique_recipients, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by sender, subject
| where email_count >= 3
| sort -email_count
| table sender, subject, email_count, unique_recipients, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 3: Behavioral Detection - Help desk social engineering attack
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1621
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 4: Behavioral Detection - Real-time phishing relay (AiTM) attack
``` Category: initial_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1566
index=main sourcetype=mail action=delivered (attachment_count>0 OR url_count>0)
| stats count as email_count, dc(recipient) as unique_recipients, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by sender, subject
| where email_count >= 3
| sort -email_count
| table sender, subject, email_count, unique_recipients, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 5: Behavioral Detection - MFA fatigue attack
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1078
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 6: Behavioral Detection - Cloud-synced passkey compromise chain
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 7: Behavioral Detection - Device theft to authentication bypass
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1

compiled_outputs.sigma:
# KTLYST Detection Rules (SIGMA format)
# Generated: 7 rules from 7 indicators
# Convert to platform-native with: sigma convert -t <target>
---

title: 'KTLYST - Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain'
id: e77a17d4-bf8c-5405-99c5-01be701f4643
status: experimental
description: |
    Auto-generated detection rule.
    Source provenance: Page 1
level: medium
author: KTLYST
date: 2026/03/24
references:
    - 'KTLYST source: Page 1'
tags:
    - attack.detection
    - attack.t1078
logsource:
    category: authentication
    product: any
detection:
    selection:
        status: 'failure'
    filter_low_count:
        count|gte: 5
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative activity

---

title: 'KTLYST - AI-assisted targeted phishing campaign'
id: eba34a93-e341-5e48-8313-9209886b04e6
status: experimental
description: |
    Auto-generated detection rule.

    - attack.detection

logsource:
    category: mail
    product: any
detection:

        action: 'delivered'
    filter_attachments:
        attachment_count|gte: 1
    condition: selection and filter_attachments
